title: senecio dimorphocarpos
intro: Senecio dimorphocarpos, also known as the 'blue spurrey', is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Europe, due to its rich content of sesquiterpene lactones, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Senecio dimorphocarpos are believed to provide a range of health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Senecio dimorphocarpos can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
